key_insight: RL post-training gains are concentrated in a small number of transformer layers, and in many cases a single middle layer can recover most of the benefit of full-parameter training.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a far more parameter-efficient and cheaper RL fine-tuning strategy for LLMs, with potential benefits for training speed, memory use, and targeted adaptation.
method: Train one transformer layer at a time while keeping the rest of the model frozen.
Define layer contribution as the fraction of full RL improvement recovered by an isolated layer.
Compare layer rankings across model families, RL algorithms, and task domains.
evidence_signal: The study evaluates seven models across Qwen3 and Qwen2.5, three RL algorithms, and multiple tasks, finding a stable middle-layer concentration pattern and strongly correlated layer rankings.
watch_item: Layer importance appears model- and task-dependent enough that the best layer should be validated empirically before using a single-layer training recipe.
paper_title: Is One Layer Enough? Training A Single Transformer Layer Can Match Full-Parameter RL Training
